task_completion.description: Import the gs-maven project into Eclipse
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Project Explorer or Package Explorer view shows the 'gs-maven' project loaded.

task_completion.description: Set a breakpoint on the specified line in HelloWorld.java
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Eclipse editor for HelloWorld.java, there is a blue dot (breakpoint indicator) in the left margin next to the line 'System.out.println(greeter.sayHello());'.

task_completion.description: Run and debug the HelloWorld main class, pausing at the breakpoint
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eclipse is in the Debug perspective, and execution is paused at the breakpoint line (the line is highlighted, usually in green or with an instruction pointer arrow).

task_completion.description: Inspect the 'greeter' variable during the debug session
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'greeter' variable is visible in the Eclipse Variables view (typically in the top right of the Debug perspective), or there is a hover tooltip showing its details in the code editor.

task_completion.description: Step through the remaining execution until the program completes
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Console view shows the final program output (including the time and greeting), and the Debug view shows the HelloWorld application process as '<terminated>'.

integrity.description: Agent used Eclipse IDE for the task
integrity.visual_evidence: All actions (importing, debugging, inspecting) occur within the Eclipse IDE GUI, not in a terminal or a different text editor.

integrity.description: Agent actually used the Eclipse debugger
integrity.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Debug perspective is active with genuine UI elements like the Variables view, Debug view, and stepping controls, rather than mocked text or terminal debuggers like jdb.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass Eclipse by running the code in a terminal
integrity.visual_evidence: The program output appears in the Eclipse Console view, not in a standalone terminal emulator window running Maven or Java commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/gs-maven. The main class is hello.HelloWorld. The breakpoint must be set on 'System.out.println(greeter.sayHello());' in src/main/java/hello/HelloWorld.java. The final console output will display the current local time (via Joda-Time) followed by a greeting.
